AM OpEx | Intraday post (15/Jan)

Weekly post:

January OpEx week quant breakdown | Weekly post (12-16/Jan) - FREE

Last weekly post upside target was hit.

The weekly post is entirely free. It contains the key levels, and expected dynamics and my derivation of sentiment from the vol markets.

No structure was broken, market behaves just as the weekly post predicted (some profit taking has occured earlier). Rangebounding, liquidity hunting is happening with positive drift, and market is running on mechanicals as I said in November and December that it will. No bulls and bears get momentum exhausting both sides.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

The speed profile is net negative, and it’s concentrating more on protecting the downside rather than spot up/vol up momentum expectations, especially as vvol is pretty much sold. Net gamma is positive. This can cause some downside drag.

Centroid is at 6884.20

Downside pivot is at 6857.68with target of 6842.15I would say margin of error of 6828 (including vvol expectations).This is a very supportive area and locally quite sticky.

Upside pivot is at 6951.84with target of 7029.55 that would 1) pin the price with lower probability, than 2) reject the price back down to 6990 and pin there. The upside pivot however also functionates as a line in the sand, and there is a very high probability that spot will be rejected there. Downward from there, there are some strong support zones 6930/6910, the centroid and then the downside pivots.

On the 0DTE front, the market is priced to be sticky into both direction, but given, we are below the upside pivot, the odds favor for downside-ish trend. Not bearishness, but a downside drag on the spot. If the upside pivot would be broken during the overnight session, or in the first 2 hours, price can stabilize there. However, structurally the ES says bulls are in control. The key is that either way the trend is rangebound with positive drift.I only see momentum edge if spot is below the upside pivot.And I see premium collecting edge anyway.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
